title: It’s Happening... Anthropic MYTHOS 1 Is Here!
summary: Anthropic has developed MYTHOS 1, a specialized AI model for discovering software vulnerabilities and exploit chains. The model discovered thousands of serious bugs in open-source projects during Project Glasswing and is being integrated into Claude Code and Claude Security products.
writeup: This video covers Anthropic's Project Glass Wing, in which Claude Mythos 1 found over 10,000 high-severity or critical vulnerabilities across roughly 50 major infrastructure companies in 30 days, with a false positive rate below that of human security testers. The central tension is the patching bottleneck: AI discovers vulnerabilities orders of magnitude faster than human maintainers can fix them, with only 75 of 1,129 submitted vulnerabilities patched so far. A parallel thread scrutinizes Anthropic's financial reporting, examining discrepancies between ARR claims, CFO sworn testimony, and the timing of a discounted SpaceX Colossus compute deal that suppresses costs during the quarter being cited as the company's first profitable one.
key_points: Project Glass Wing used Claude Mythos to scan over 1,000 open-source projects, identifying 23,019 vulnerabilities total; after independent verification by six security firms, 1,094 were confirmed high-severity or critical with a 90.6% true positive rate, and the false positive rate was lower than top human testers.

Mythos achieved results qualitatively beyond prior models: Mozilla Firefox 150 received patches for 271 critical vulnerabilities in a single pass, more than 10 times what Opus 4.6 found in Firefox 148, and Mythos autonomously constructed a complete exploit chain for a 27-year-old hidden bug in OpenBSD with no human assistance.

The WolfSSL case illustrates the offensive capability: Mythos found a vulnerability in a cryptography library running on billions of IoT, router, and smart-car devices, then independently wrote attack code capable of forging digital certificates to create convincing fake banking and login sites.

The bottleneck has inverted from discovery to remediation: human programmers average two weeks to fix a single high-severity vulnerability, open-source maintainers sent Anthropic pleading emails to slow down, and only 75 of 1,129 submitted vulnerabilities have been patched so far; Claude Security, Anthropic's enterprise patch-generation tool, fixed over 2,100 vulnerabilities in its first three weeks.

Mythos 1 strings appeared in Claude Code and Claude Security source code the day after Anthropic publicly stated the model would remain restricted, suggesting a faster rollout than officially acknowledged; the UK AI Safety Institute confirmed Mythos preview is the first model to defeat their dual network challenge end to end.

Anthropic's claimed first profitable quarter, citing operating profit of $559 million on projected Q2 revenue of $10.9 billion, is complicated by a $1.25 billion per month SpaceX Colossus compute deal whose discounted rate applies specifically to Q2, and by revenue figures that are difficult to reconcile with the CFO's sworn testimony of revenues exceeding $5 billion to date.
